content:
# Well Log Analysis

**Summary**: The measurement of physical properties of rocks and fluids in a borehole using electrical or nuclear tools.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[2001-al-khalifa|Al-Khalifa, 2001]] used well-log-analysis to provide the primary data for porosity modeling as it is more widely available than core samples.

- [[1997-al-marhoun|Al-Marhoun, 1997]] used well-log-analysis to provide high vertical resolution data for reservoir property estimation.

- [[1993-moallim|Moallim, 1993]] used well-log-analysis to interpret lithological logs for stratigraphic review and aquifer identification.

- [[1997-al-qassab|Al-Qassab, 1997]] used well-log-analysis to derive Vsh, porosity, and lithofacies for the SFNC sequence.

- [[1996-al-salem|Al-Salem, 1996]] used well-log analysis to derive porosity values from 44 wells in the Harm1yah field.

- [[2002-siddiqui|Siddiqui, 2002]] used well-log-analysis to interpret the Unayzah Formation in three fields.

- [[2003-al-shridi|Al-Shridi, 2003]] used well-log-analysis to derive porosity data from sonic, neutron, and density logs for the Arab-D reservoir.

- [[1992-al-sulami|Al-Sulami, 1992]] used well-log-analysis to establish and correlate major stratigraphic divisions in the Thamama Group.

- [[2009-sitouah|Sitouah, 2009]] used well-log-analysis to provide input data for the neural network models.

- [[2010-alroudhan|AlRoudhan, 2010]] applied well-log-analysis to integrate core data with log responses for petrophysical rock typing.

- [[2010-al-mojel|Al-Mojel, 2010]] used well-log-analysis to correlate parasequences across multiple wells in the Khurais field.

- [[2011-busubait|Busubait, 2011]] used well-log-analysis to correlate the Arab-C and Sub-C units.

- [[2012-iwalewa|Iwalewa, 2012]] applied well-log-analysis to define the productive zones of the UER and Rus formations.

- [[2012-abdelgader|Abdelgader, 2012]] used FMI-logs from five wells to identify fracture orientation and geometry.

- [[2012-ghazwani|Ghazwani, 2012]] used well-log analysis via delta Log R to identify source rock zones from gamma ray, density, and resistivity logs.

- [[2012-attyah|Attyah, 2012]] used well-log-analysis to understand the context of drilling operations and waste generation in the Eastern Province.

- [[2013-mark|Mark, 2013]] used mud-weight data from three wells as the primary training dataset for the SVM algorithm.

- [[2013-abdullah|Abdullah, 2013]] applied well-log-analysis to compare standard methods against LSA techniques.

- [[2013-el-kheir|El-Kheir, 2013]] used well log analysis to calculate shale volume, porosity, and water saturation.

- [[2013-atig|atig, 2013]] used well-log-analysis to identify facies in the Safaniya member and perform upscaling for 3D modeling.

- [[2013-abdalla|Abdalla, 2013]] used SGR (Spectral Gamma Ray) logging on the outcrop to model 3D distribution of K, Th, and U.

- [[2014-kola|Kola, 2014]] applied well-log-analysis to VSP data to extract transmission amplitudes.

- [[2014-mohammed|Mohammed, 2014]] used core plug measurements to determine total porosity and permeability for each lithofacies.

- [[2014-sami|Sami, 2014]] used well-log-analysis via SGR tools to characterize the mineral content of the outcrop.

- [[2015-mustafa|Mustafa, 2015]] used well-log-analysis (MSFL, Density, S-wave) to identify open and mineralized fractures in the Qusaiba Shale.

- [[2015-fuad|Fuad, 2015]] used well-log-analysis to provide training data for the neural network models.

- [[2015-own|Own, 2015]] used well-log-analysis (specifically gamma-ray) to identify source rock quality in the Qusaiba Member.

- [[2015-jabir|Jabir, 2015]] used well-log-analysis to identify electro-facies and calculate porosity, permeability, and water saturation.

- [[2015-abdlmutalib|Abdlmutalib, 2015]] used core analysis to measure porosity and permeability in the Arab-D member.

- [[2015-elzain|Elzain, 2015]] used SGR logging to correlate lithofacies with mineralogical and chemical signatures.

- [[2015-sanuade|Sanuade, 2015]] used well-log-analysis to identify 14 reservoir sands and characterize facies in the Otu field.

- [[2016-hassan|Hassan, 2016]] used well log analysis (specifically borehole image logs) to validate AVOA results.

- [[2016-lawal|Lawal, 2016]] used well-log-analysis to evaluate elastic constants and brittleness indicators in shale samples.

- [[2016-awad|Awad, 2016]] used well-log-analysis including GR, SP, and Density-Neutron to identify lithofacies in the Samma, Yabus, and Adar formations.

- [[2016-ejaz|Ejaz, 2016]] used well-log-analysis to develop electrofacies models for the Sarah Formation.

- [[2016-saleh|Saleh, 2016]] applied well-log-analysis to calculate dynamic elastic moduli from compressional/shear slowness and bulk density.

- [[2016-ali|Ali, 2016]] reviewed well log data as a standard method for identifying pore fluids before proposing the seismic inversion alternative.

- [[2016-houssain|Houssain, 2016]] used GWLA to enhance and condition the quality of elastic logs for a clastic reservoir.

- [[2017-mahgoub|Mahgoub, 2017]] used spectral gamma ray tools in the field to identify lithofacies.

- [[2017-mohammed|Mohammed, 2017]] used well log analysis for detailed petrophysical evaluation including porosity, permeability, and hydrocarbon saturation.

- [[2017-mohammed-2|Mohammed, 2017]] used SGR to measure K, U, and Th for lithofacies identification and correlation.

- [[2017-yousif|Yousif, 2017]] used petrophysical analysis to determine the flow zone indicators (FZI) for the Dhruma Formation.

- [[2017-abdelgader|Abdelgader, 2017]] used well-log-analysis (Gamma Ray) to identify lithofacies and calculate sand/shale percentages in the Abu Gabra Formation.

- [[2019-al-dawood|Al-Dawood, 2019]] used well- logs for rock physics modeling and feasibility studies.

- [[2020-abdallah|Abdallah, 2020]] used well-log-analysis (GR, RHOB, NPHI) as primary inputs for the ANN models.

- [[2020-al-zoukani|Al-Zoukani, 2020]] used well-log-analysis principles to relate lithology and petrophysics.

- [[2020-almumtin|Almumtin, 2020]] used wireline data to establish the initial parameters for the Rock Physics Model (RPM).

- [[2020-gbiyanju|Gbiyanju, 2020]] used well-log-analysis to calibrate rock elastic properties (REP) with core measurements for better upscaling.

- [[2021-almashhor|AlMashhor, 2021]] applied well-log-analysis to compare MDPE and DBSCAN methods for automated picking.

- [[2021-albensaad|Albensaad, 2021]] applied well-log-analysis to establish rock strength profiles and compare them with core-based results.

- [[2021-siddiq|Siddiq, 2021]] used SIP for real-time monitoring of arsenic removal.

- [[2021-al-malki|Al-Malki, 2021]] utilized real-time data from the DRU system and Saudi Aramco's platform to monitor hole cleaning.

- [[2022-syahputra|Syahputra, 2022]] used well-log-analysis to map the spatial distribution of burrowed strata.

- [[2023-kusuma|Kusuma, 2023]] applied well-log-analysis to predict missing porosity and facies data from 1D log sets.

- [[2023-alzayer|Alzayer, 2023]] used well-log-analysis data (resistivity, GR, density, sonic) as inputs for the machine learning models.

- [[2023-gad|Gad, 2023]] used well logs (resistivity, GR, density, sonic, neutron) as inputs for ANN models.

- [[2024-alabbad|Alabbad, 2024]] used well-log-analysis to characterize carbonate reservoirs and organic-rich mudrocks.

- [[2024-allam|Allam, 2024]] applied well-log-analysis to evaluate the reservoir characteristics of the Valanginian carbonates.

- [[2024-hassan|Hassan, 2024]] used well-log-analysis to correlate core data with log responses (GR, NPHI, RHOB).

- [[2024-yusmananto|Yusmananto, 2024]] used well-log_analysis to characterize the Upper Kharaib Member (UKM) reservoir properties.

- [[2024-fahmi|Fahmi, 2024]] used well-log-analysis from the Riyadh and Khurais wells to calibrate the model.

- [[2024-maigana|Maigana, 2024]] used well-log-analysis to characterize reservoir property and connectivity in the Taranaki Basin.

- [[2024-akomolafe|Akomolafe, 2024]] applied well-log-analysis to interpret NMR T2 responses in the context of formation evaluation.

- [[2025-al-afif|Al-Afif, 2025]] processed meteorological data using CALMET to generate surface friction velocity and other parameters.

- [[1992-abdulghani|Abdulghani, 1992]] applied well-log-analysis to correlate units between wells 587 and 628.

- [[2002-al-ali|Al-Ali, 2002]] used uphole surveys to measure direct P-wave velocity ($V_p$) in near-surface layers.

- [[2002-elhassan|ElHassan, 2002]] applied well-log-analysis including FMI for in-situ stress orientation.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- Well logs are used as the base for porosity modeling because they are available at most wells and offer a larger volume of investigation compared to core plugs. ([[2001-al-khalifa|Al-Khalifa, 2001, p. 31]])

- Integration of well information with seismic data is essential due to the differing spatial and vertical resolutions of each data type. ([[1997-al-marhoun|Al-Marhoun, 1997, p. 92]])

- Lithological logs were used to identify the thickness and facies of formations such as the Baidoa, Hamantey, and Sawer formations. ([[1993-moallim|Moallim, 1993, p. 24]])

- Lithofacies were determined using a suite of logs including Density, Neutron, and Gamma ray with specific cutoffs. ([[1997-al-qassab|Al-Qassab, 1997, p. 50]])

- Well-log derived porosity was used as the primary input for both kriging and simulation models. ([[1996-al-salem|Al-Salem, 1996, p. 19]])

- Gamma ray (GR), Sonic (DT), and Bulk Density (RB) logs were used to distinguish between sandstone and shale/mudstone units and identify depositional motifs. ([[2002-siddiqui|Siddiqui, 2002, p. 110]])

- All wells have porosity logs derived from sonic, neutron, and density logs. ([[2003-al-shridi|Al-Shridi, 2003, p. 18]])

- Well-log data (gamma ray, sonic, formation-density, and neutron) were used to define the boundaries of the Sulaiy, Yamama, Buwaib/Biyadh, and Shu'aiba formations. ([[1992-al-sulami|Al-Sulami, 1992, p. 24]])

- Well log data combined with seismic attributes were used to train and test both MLP and GRNN models. ([[2009-sitouah|Sitouah, 2009, p. 15]])

- Well logs were used to identify petrophysical rocks in uncored wells by correlating them to gamma ray, density, neutron, and sonic logs. ([[2010-alroudhan|AlRoudhan, 2010, p. 70]])

- Gamma-ray logs were used to distinguish between shale minerals (high gamma) and limestone minerals (low gamma). ([[2010-al-mojel|Al-Mojel, 2010, p. 43]])

- Used to define the structural boundaries and thickness of the reservoir units. ([[2011-busubait|Busubait, 2011, p. 31]])

- Well log data from KFUPM were used to determine that the UER aquifer has a mean thickness of 400 m regionally but approximately 85 m in the local study area. ([[2012-iwalewa|Iwalewa, 2012, p. 54]])

- Fractures observed from the FMI-log of five wells showed similar trends to faults detected from seismic data. ([[2012-abdelgader|Abdelgader, 2012, p. 11]])

- The delta Log R method successfully identified the Hanadir source rock zone across three wells (A, B, and C). ([[2012-ghazwani|Ghazwani, 2012, p. 56]])

- Not specifically quantified in this thesis as it is used for general context. ([[2012-attyah|Attyah, 2012]])

- Mud-weight data proved to be indicative of the pore pressure conditions of the target formations. ([[2013-mark|Mark, 2013, p. 104]])

- Standard well-log-analysis underestimates hydrocarbon saturation in thin-bedded sands due to the averaging effect of low-resolution tools. ([[2013-abdullah|Abdullah, 2013, p. 16]])

- Petrophysical parameters were calculated using a combination of sonic, density, and neutron logs. ([[2013-el-kheir|El-Kheir, 2013, p. 39]])

- Well log analysis allowed for the identification of three main facies: fluvial channel, bay, and marine. ([[2013-atig|atig, 2013, p. 44]])

- SGR log motifs were successfully used to identify reservoir zonation and lithofacies associations. ([[2013-abdalla|Abdalla, 2013, p. 171]])

- Utilized VSP geometry with receivers below the target horizon for TAVO analysis. ([[2014-kola|Kola, 2014, p. 15]])

- Core porosity in the oolitic grainstone ranged from 25% to 35%. ([[2014-mohammed|Mohammed, 2014, p. 95]])

- SGR logs for U, Th, and K were used to differentiate between various carbonate facies. ([[2014-sami|Sami, 2014, p. 57]])

- S-wave travel time increased significantly due to the presence of fractures. ([[2015-mustafa|Mustafa, 2015, p. 45]])

- Well logs (density and neutron) were used as the primary ground truth for calibrating seismic attributes. ([[2015-fuad|Fuad, 2015, p. 19]])

- A cutoff value of 150 API gamma-ray units distinguishes potential source shale from non-source shale. ([[2015-own|Own, 2015, p. 34]])

- Well logging curves were used to classify three distinct lithofacies (sand, shaly-sand, shale) and evaluate petrophysical properties. ([[2015-jabir|Jabir, 2015, p. 13]])

- Average porosity was 2.1% with a maximum of 5.4%, while average permeability was 0.2 md. ([[2015-abdlmutalib|Abdlmutalib, 2015, p. 90]])

- SGR signatures showed moderate correlation with lithofacies for each high-frequency sequence. ([[2015-elzain|Elzain, 2015]])

- Identified four distinct log patterns: blocky (sandstone), coarsening upward (shaly sandstone), serrated (mudrock), and bell-shaped (heterolith). ([[2015-sanuade|Sanuade, 2015, p. 60]])

- Unayzah-C appears as chaotic and irregular with no or very weak bedding on borehole image logs. ([[2016-hassan|Hassan, 2016, p. 10]])

- Identified (E/k) as the most robust brittle indicator among several log-derived parameters. ([[2016-lawal|Lawal, 2016, p. 58]])

- GR log motifs were used to define nine distinct lithofacies associations across three formations. ([[2016-awad|Awad, 2016, p. 17]])

- Well log data were used in multivariate statistical methods (K-means, PCA) to identify distinct lithofacies and boundaries. ([[2016-ejaz|Ejaz, 2016, p. 13]])

- Derived four dynamic elastic moduli (G, K, E, v) for the entire Khuff Formation. ([[2016-saleh|Saleh, 2016, p. 30]])

- Not stated in source ([[2016-ali|Ali, 2016]])

- GWLA was used to determine a rock physics model by improving raw data from gamma ray, density, resistivity, caliper, and sonic logs. ([[2016-houssain|Houssain, 2016, p. 17]])

- High gamma-ray responses indicated high organic content in the fissile shale. ([[2017-mahgoub|Mahgoub, 2017, p. 64]])

- Used to identify reservoir and non-reservoir facies based on porosity, permeability, and hydrocarbon saturation. ([[2017-mohammed|Mohammed, 2017, p. 30]])

- Used as a tool for identifying lithofacies and providing a regional correlation guide. ([[2017-mohammed-2|Mohammed, 2017, p. 41]])

- Nine hydraulic flow units were established with FZI values ranging from 0.0938 to 1.5. ([[2017-yousif|Yousif, 2017, p. 20]])

- Gamma ray logs were used to distinguish between sandstone-dominated (yellow) and mudstone-dominated (green) units in braided delta systems. ([[2017-abdelgader|Abdelgader, 2017, p. 142]])

- Well log cross plots were used to determine pay zones through gas fluid substitution using the Gassmann equation. ([[2019-al-dawood|Al-Dawood, 2019, p. 15]])

- Integration of GR, RHOB, and NPHI logs allowed for the prediction of porosity with an AAPE of 5.94. ([[2020-abdallah|Abdallah, 2020, p. 17]])

- Used the cementation exponent (m) derived from log-based equations to characterize rock types. ([[2020-al-zoukani|Al-Zoukani, 2020, p. 120]])

- Wireline data from 8 wells provided both sonic and dipole data, density, and mineralogical interpretation. ([[2020-almumtin|Almumtin, 2020, p. 18]])

- Calibration of well log REP with core measurements is essential for geomechanical modeling in unconventional reservoirs. ([[2020-gbiyanju|Gbiyanju, 2020, p. 33]])

- First arrival traveltime is essential to be picked accurately to enhance the efficiency of static correction. ([[2021-almashhor|AlMashhor, 2021, p. 14]])

- Log-based UCS showed higher strength across the tested interval compared to scratch test values, potentially leading to overestimation in models. ([[2021-albensaad|Albensaad, 2021, p. 33]])

- SIP monitoring showed changes in signals consistent with arsenic removal confirmed by chemical analysis. ([[2021-siddiq|Siddiq, 2021, p. 13]])

- Real-time monitoring of YP, PV, ROP, and ECD allows for immediate adjustment of drilling parameters to maintain HCF above 1.0. ([[2021-al-malki|Al-Malki, 2021, p. 42]])

- The burrowed stratum has a laterally similar spatial stratigraphic pattern for ~30 km. ([[2022-syahputra|Syahputra, 2022, p. 81]])

- ANN, RF, and SVM were the primary models used for 1D prediction from well logs. ([[2023-kusuma|Kusuma, 2023, p. 20]])

- Data points included temperature, porosity, and water saturation derived from logs to predict pore pressure. ([[2023-alzayer|Alzayer, 2023, p. 13]])

- Well-logs provide continuous data across the depth of the reservoir, unlike core analysis which provides discrete points. ([[2023-gad|Gad, 2023, p. 15]])

- Wireline data (GR, bulk density, Vp, Vs) provided the basis for rock physics diagnostics in both carbonate and shale systems. ([[2024-alabbad|Alabbad, 2024, p. 101]])

- Not stated in source ([[2024-allam|Allam, 2024]])

- Core results showed good agreement with wireline logs regarding calcite-dolomite segregation and acoustic variation. ([[2024-hassan|Hassan, 2024, p. 14]])

- Well log correlation analysis across all wells identified distinct facies associations (FA1-FA4). ([[2024-yusmananto|Yusmananto, 2024, p. 40]])

- Well data were used to validate the simulated lithologies and facies with high accuracy. ([[2024-fahmi|Fahmi, 2024, p. 65]])

- Petrophysical analysis showed average effective porosity of 15-22% for the reservoir and 3-5% for the seal. ([[2024-maigana|Maigana, 2024, p. 12]])

- Not stated in source (specific results are in Chapter 4). ([[2024-akomolafe|Akomolafe, 2024]])

- Meteorological inputs were sourced from three regional stations within a 150-km radius. ([[2025-al-afif|Al-Afif, 2025, p. 31]])

- Correlation in the two wells was achieved using core descriptions, well logs, and thin section analysis. ([[1992-abdulghani|Abdulghani, 1992, p. 1]])

- Uphole surveys provide the primary ground truth for $V_p$ values used in the correlation study. ([[2002-al-ali|Al-Ali, 2002, p. 45]])

- The azimuths of the minimum and maximum horizontal stress are approximately northwest and northeast, respectively. ([[2002-elhassan|ElHassan, 2002, p. 19]])

## Methodological Variations Across Sources


## Technical Limitations and Open Questions


## Related Concepts

**Related Techniques**: 

**Applicable to Terminology**: 

**Used in Themes**:
